title: Source Registration, Subscriptions, Following, and Ownership · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds shared-source and tenant subscription model from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of shared-source and tenant subscription model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Register a discovered source idempotently from normalized identity, provenance, scope decision, and initial fetch evidence.
Create and remove tenant-scoped subscriptions idempotently while preserving a shared publisher source and entry history.
Model pending, active, paused, moved, failing, disabled, deleted, and appeal states with allowed transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Source Registration
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 5198 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile shared source identity, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle, authorization, and registration races; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Source Registration.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for register a discovered source idempotently from normalized identity, provenance, scope decision, and initial fetch evidence. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Source Registration, keep Feed Go Source Registration connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
